perturbation.original_text: b) That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit.
perturbation.changed_text: b) That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study; however, the participant retains the right to use their own ideas for personal projects.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the company owns the ideas generated, but simultaneously grants the participant the right to use those same ideas for personal projects. This contradicts the initial assertion of complete ownership by the company, creating uncertainty about the enforcement of intellectual property rights. This in-text contradiction causes uncertainty on the ownership of the ideas and suggestions contributed in the discussion. The contradiction resides on Section b.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study; however, the participant retains the right to use their own ideas for personal projects.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that participants retain the right to use their own ideas for personal projects, despite the preceding clause stating that all ideas conceived during the Focus Group shall be the property of the company. This directly contradicts the initial assertion of complete ownership, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text and Change Text are the same, but the change introduces a contradiction within itself, causing uncertainty about the ownership of the ideas and suggestions.

perturbation.original_text: d) That you, shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential a nd not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information beyond those ac tivities that are part of the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: d) That you, shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential a nd not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information; however, you may disclose to close family members for advisory purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by initially stating that Confidential Information cannot be disclosed to any third party, but then allowing disclosure to close family members. This creates uncertainty about what constitutes a permissible disclosure and weakens the confidentiality agreement. This change is contradicting the initial assertion of complete confidentiality by allowing the disclosure of confidential information to close family members for advisory purposes. The contradiction resides on Section d.
perturbation.contradicted_text: d) That you, shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential a nd not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information; however, you may disclose to close family members for advisory purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section d by initially prohibiting disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party. However, it then introduces an exception, allowing disclosure to close family members for advisory purposes. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating they are from the same section. Despite being in the same section, the addition of the exception directly contradicts the initial statement of complete confidentiality, therefore, the change introduces a contradiction within the section.

perturbation.original_text: f) Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: f) Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person, except for their first name and general location. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds an exception, stating that first names and general locations are not considered PII, while simultaneously defining PII as information that could reasonably allow identification of a person. This introduces a contradiction because a first name and general location could, in some circumstances, allow identification. The contradictory exception of the first name and general location from the definintion of Personally Identifiable Information. The contradiction resides on Section f.
perturbation.contradicted_text: f) Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person, except for their first name and general location. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of PII by adding an exception for "first name and general location." The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but this doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction. The perturbation explanation highlights that this exception contradicts the broader definition of PII as "information that might reasonably allow identification of the person" because first names and general locations can, in some instances, reasonably allow for identification. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies causing in-text contradictions, where different sections provide conflicting obligations, definitions or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being sufficiently compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s).
perturbation.changed_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being sufficiently compensated for your participation in this Focus Group; however, the company reserves the right to edit your opinions to align with their marketing strategy, and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. The original text states that the opinions provided are solely the participant's own. The altered text then asserts the company's right to edit those opinions. This creates a contradiction because it is now unclear whether the participant's opinions truly remain their own. This in-text contradiction causes uncertanty on the opinions being provided being solely the participant's own. The contradiction resides on Section g.
perturbation.contradicted_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being sufficiently compensated for your participation in this Focus Group; however, the company reserves the right to edit your opinions to align with their marketing strategy, and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that opinions are solely the participant's own. The changed text gives the company the right to edit those opinions. This directly contradicts the notion that the opinions are solely the participant's own, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition provided in the prompt. The contradiction lies within section 'g' as indicated in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: h) That you, hereby give permission to the company sponsoring this research study for an audio recording to be made of this session. That you understand a tr anscription of this tape may be used by the company sponsoring t his study for research purposes only.
perturbation.changed_text: h) That you, hereby give permission to the company sponsoring this research study for an audio recording to be made of this session. That you understand a tr anscription of this tape may be used by the company sponsoring t his study for research and marketing purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the earlier statement that the transcription will be used for research purposes only. By adding 'marketing purposes,' it broadens the scope of use and potentially violates the initial understanding of the agreement. This causes an in-text contradiction regarding what the usage of the transcription being made, since there is a clear change in the scope usage and purpose being utilized. The contradiction resides on Section h.
perturbation.contradicted_text: h) That you, hereby give permission to the company sponsoring this research study for an audio recording to be made of this session. That you understand a tr anscription of this tape may be used by the company sponsoring t his study for research and marketing purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the transcription will be used for 'research purposes only'. The changed text expands the scope to include 'research and marketing purposes'. This directly contradicts the initial limitation, as 'marketing purposes' introduces a new, previously disallowed usage. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed text, as the original intention for just research now has a broader purpose that includes marketing. According to the perturbation explanation, this broadened scope of usage for transcription, violates the initial agreement creating an in-text contradiction.
